sections.heading: Power of the Cabinet Secretary to issue rules and regulations ......................................................................................... 15
sections.title: Cabinet Secretary's power to issue capital markets rules
sections.summary: The Cabinet Secretary may issue rules regulating securities listing, disclosures by market participants, record-keeping, capital market operations, foreign investor participation, investment schemes, venture capital, credit ratings, securities trading, clearing, forex activities, fund managers, and financial penalties. Rules must promote an effective securities market and be publicly exposed for 30 days for stakeholder feedback.
sections.dtia_summary: Rules require public consultation before implementation and define regulatory oversight for capital markets activities, impacting cross-border data flows through market participant requirements.

requirements.text: Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for securities listing and de-listing
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for disclosures by stockbrokers, derivatives brokers, and persons acquiring/disposing securities
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for record-keeping by licensed persons
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for capital market operations
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for foreign investor participation
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for collective investment schemes
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for registered venture capital companies
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for credit rating agencies
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for securities trading in Kenya
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for securities clearing and settlement organizations
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for online forex trading activities
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for fund managers
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for investment banks
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for authorized securities dealers
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for self-regulatory organizations
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for use of funds from securities issues
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for financial penalties for non-compliance
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for annual fees by exchanges
Cabinet Secretary shall formulate rules for disclosure requirements on listings

sections.source_quote: The Cabinet Secretary shall formulate such rules and regulations as may be required to regulate—

sections.heading: Furnishing of information to the Authority .............................................................................................................................. 18
sections.title: Information disclosure to Authority
sections.summary: The Authority may require persons to provide information via written notice within specified timeframes. The Authority may not disclose information except for its objectives unless required by court. The Authority may share information with foreign regulatory bodies under specific conditions including public interest and reciprocal assistance.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires data sharing with regulatory authorities and cross-border cooperation with foreign regulators under defined conditions, impacting data transfer impact assessments.

requirements.text: Authority may require persons to furnish information via written notice
Authority may not disclose information except for its objectives unless court requires
Authority may share information with foreign regulatory bodies for investigations
Foreign regulatory bodies must demonstrate public interest for assistance requests
Foreign regulatory bodies must be willing to reciprocate assistance

sections.source_quote: The Authority or any person officially authorized in that behalf by the Authority may, by notice in writing, require any person to furnish to the Authority or to the authorized person, within such period as is specified in the notice, all such returns or information as specified in such notice.

sections.heading: Establishment of the Investor Compensation Fund ............................................................................................................... 21
sections.title: Investor Compensation Fund establishment for stockbroker failures
sections.summary: This section establishes the Investor Compensation Fund to compensate investors who suffer financial loss due to licensed stockbrokers or dealers failing to meet contractual obligations. The Fund is funded by contributions from licensed persons, fines, penalties, interest, recovered sums, and approved sources. The Authority may reward individuals who provide timely information leading to Fund recovery, with rewards capped at five million shillings. Offenses related to fraudulently obtaining rewards can result in fines up to five million shillings or imprisonment for five years.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes a fund for compensating investors affected by stockbroker failures, which may impact data transfer DTIAs if the Fund's operations involve cross-border data handling or international investor compensation mechanisms.

requirements.text: There shall be established a Fund to be known as the Investor Compensation Fund for the purposes of granting compensation to investors who suffer pecuniary loss resulting from the failure of a licensed stockbroker or dealer to meet his contractual obligations.
The Compensation Fund shall consist of such moneys as are required to be paid into the Compensation Fund by licensed persons
The Compensation Fund shall consist of such sums of money as are paid under this Act as fines or penalties or under section 34 as ill-gotten gains where those harmed are not specifically identifiable
The Compensation Fund shall consist of such sums of money as accrue from interest and profits from investing Compensation Fund moneys

The Compensation Fund shall consist of such sums of money recovered by or on behalf of the Authority from entities whose failure to meet their obligations to investors result in payments from the Compensation Fund
The Authority may reward any person who provides new and timely information leading to the recovery of sums of money referred to in subsection (2)
The reward payable under this subsection shall be three per cent of the amount recovered subject to a maximum of five million shillings
The reward referred to in paragraph (a) shall be paid before the recovered sums of money are transferred to the Fund
A person who colludes with an officer of the Authority for the purpose of collecting the reward under subsection (2A) commits an offence
A person who while working at the Authority aids another person to get and provide information under subsection (2A) commits an offence
A person who provides false information under subsection (2A) commits an offence
A person who commits an offence under subsection (2C) shall on conviction be liable to a fine not exceeding five million shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years

sections.source_quote: 18. Establishment of the Investor Compensation Fund (1) There shall be established a Fund to be known as the Investor Compensation Fund for the purposes of granting compensation to investors who suffer pecuniary loss resulting from the failure of a licensed stockbroker or dealer to meet his contractual obligations.

sections.heading: Disciplinary action by securities exchange ............................................................................................................................... 27
sections.title: Disciplinary action by securities exchanges
sections.summary: Securities exchanges must notify the Authority within seven days of taking disciplinary action against trading participants or listed companies. The Authority may review such actions, affirm or set aside decisions after providing opportunities for hearing. The Authority can also take disciplinary action itself if an exchange fails to act, but must give prior notice.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires exchanges to report disciplinary actions to the regulator; regulator can override exchange decisions; regulator may directly discipline participants if exchanges fail to act.

requirements.text: Securities exchanges must notify Authority within seven days of disciplinary action against trading participants or listed companies
Authority may review disciplinary actions taken by exchanges after providing opportunity for hearing
Authority may take disciplinary action if exchange fails to act against participants

sections.source_quote: Where a securities exchange or a derivatives exchange reprimands, fines, suspends or expels, or otherwise takes disciplinary action against a trading participant or a listed company, it shall within seven days give notice to the Authority in writing, giving particulars including the name of the person, the reason for and nature of the action taken.

sections.heading: Procedure for collective investment schemes .......................................................................................................................... 38
sections.title: Procedure for collective investment schemes
sections.summary: This section outlines the procedural requirements for collective investment schemes. It specifies the steps that must be followed when launching such schemes, including disclosure obligations and regulatory approvals. The process ensures transparency and compliance with capital markets regulations.

requirements.text: Collective investment schemes must follow specified procedures

sections.source_quote: 30. Procedure for collective investment schemes

sections.heading: Register of interest in securities ................................................................................................................................................... 53
sections.title: Register of interest in securities
sections.summary: This section requires the maintenance of a register of interests in securities. It mandates that entities track and disclose ownership interests to prevent insider trading and market manipulation. The register serves as a transparency mechanism for market participants.

requirements.text: Register of interest in securities must be maintained

sections.source_quote: 32. Register of interest in securities

sections.heading: Exemption from Cap. 446 ................................................................................................................................................................ 68
sections.title: Exemption from Cap. 446
sections.summary: This section provides an exemption from Cap. 446, which is likely a reference to another act or regulation. The specific exemption details are not elaborated in the text, indicating a general exemption clause without further conditions or scope.

requirements.text: Exemption from Cap. 446

sections.source_quote: 39. Exemption from Cap. 446

sections.heading: Interpretation (cont.)
sections.title: Definitions of key financial terms in capital markets
sections.summary: This section defines critical terms used in the Capital Markets Act, including 'information' related to securities trading, 'substantial shareholder', 'trading participant', 'trustee', 'underwriting', and 'unit trust'. These definitions establish the legal framework for understanding securities transactions, ownership structures, and trust arrangements within Kenya's capital markets.

requirements.text: Information is regularly provided concerning prices at which particular persons or classes propose to sell, purchase or exchange securities
Substantial shareholder means any person who is the beneficial owner of or in a position to exert control over not less than fifteen per cent of shares of a body corporate
Trading participant means a licensed person with rights to trade at an approved securities exchange
Trustee in relation to real estate investment trust or collective investment scheme means a person appointed under a trust deed as trustee
Underwriting means the purchase or commitment to purchase or distribute by dealers or other persons of issue or offer of securities for immediate or prompt public distribution
Unit trust means any scheme or arrangement in the nature of a trust where members of the public are invited to acquire an interest in specified securities

sections.source_quote: Capital Markets Act (Cap. 485A) Kenya (c) information is regularly provided concerning the prices at which, or the consideration for which, particular persons, or particular classes of persons, propose, or may reasonably be expected, to sell, purchase or exchange securities; "substantial shareholder" means any person who is the beneficial owner of, or is in a position to exert control over, not less than fifteen per cent of the shares of a body corporate; "trading participant" means a licensed person with rights to trade at an approved securities exchange; "trustee" in relation to real estate investment trust or a collective investment scheme, means a person appointed under a trust deed of a real estate investment trust or a collective investment scheme, as the case may be, as its trustee; "underwriting" means the purchase or commitment to purchase or distribute by dealers or other persons of issue or offer of securities for immediate or prompt public distribution by or through them; "unit trust" means any scheme or arrangement in the nature of a trust in pursuance whereof members of the public are invited or permitted, as beneficiaries under the trust, to acquire an interest or undivided share (unit of investment) in one or more groups or blocks of specified securities and to participate proportionately in the income or profits derived therefrom.

sections.heading: Definition of "interest in securities"
sections.title: Definition of interest in securities
sections.summary: This section defines when a person is deemed to have an interest in securities, including situations where a body corporate has an interest in a security and the person has control over the body corporate, or where the person has rights to transfer securities, options, or joint holdings. It also specifies exceptions where interests are disregarded, such as bare trusteeship or lending transactions.

requirements.text: Where any property held in trust consists of securities in which a person knows or has reasonable grounds to believe he or she has an interest, he or she shall be deemed to have an interest in those securities

A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security where a body corporate has an interest and the body corporate is accustomed or under obligation to act in accordance with directions of that person
A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security where a person has a controlling interest in the body corporate

A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security where a person is entitled to exercise or control the exercise of not less than fifteen per cent of the votes attached to voting shares in the body corporate
A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security where he or she has entered into a contract to purchase a security
A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security where he or she has a right to have a security transferred to himself or to his order
A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security where he or she has the right to acquire a security or interest in a security under an option

A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security where he or she is entitled to exercise or control the exercise of a right attached to a security, not being a security of which he or she is the registered holder
A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security if the security is held jointly with another person
An interest in a security shall not be disregarded by reason of remoteness, manner of arising, or the exercise of rights being subject to restraint or restriction
An interest in a security shall be disregarded if it is that of a person who holds the security as bare trustee
An interest in a security shall be disregarded if it is held by a person whose ordinary business includes lending money for security purposes
An interest in a security shall be disregarded if it is an interest held by a person in a prescribed office
An interest in a security shall be disregarded if it is a prescribed interest in a security

sections.source_quote: 4. Definition of "interest in securities" (1) Where any property held in trust consists of or includes securities in which a person knows, or has reasonable grounds for believing, that he or she has an interest, he or she shall be deemed to have an interest in those securities. (2) A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security where a body corporate has an interest in a security and— (a) the body corporate is, or its directors are, accustomed or under an obligation, whether formal or informal, to act in accordance with directions, instructions or wishes of that person in relation to that security; (b) that person has a controlling interest in the body corporate; or (c) that person is, or the associates of that person or that person and his associates are, entitled to exercise or control the exercise of not less than fifteen per cent of the votes attached to the voting shares in the body corporate. (3) A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security in any one or more of the following circumstances— (a) where he or she has entered into a contract to purchase a security; (b) where he or she has a right, otherwise than by reason of having an interest under a trust, to have a security transferred to himself or to his order, whether the right is exercisable presently or in the future and whether on the fulfillment of a condition or not; (c) where he or she has the right to acquire a security, or an interest in a security, under an option, whether on the fulfillment of a condition or not; or (d) where he or she is entitled, otherwise than by reason of his having been appointed a proxy or representative to vote at a meeting of members of a body corporate or of a class of its members, to exercise or control the exercise of a right attached to a security, not being a security of which he or she is the registered holder. (4) A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security if that security is held jointly with another person. (5) For the purpose of determining whether a person has an interest in a security, it is immaterial that the interest cannot be related to a particular security. (6) There shall be disregarded— (a) an interest in a security if the interest is that of a person who holds the security as bare trustee; (b) an interest in a security of a person whose ordinary business includes the lending of money if he or she holds the interest only by way of security for the purposes of a transaction entered into in the ordinary course of business in connection with the lending of money; (c) an interest of a person in a security being an interest held by him or her by reason of his holding a prescribed office; and (d) a prescribed interest in a security being an interest of such person, or of the persons included in such class of persons as is prescribed. (7) An interest in a security shall not be disregarded by reason only of— (a) its remoteness; (b) the manner in which it arose; or (c) the fact that the exercise of a right conferred by the interest is, or is capable of being made, subject to restraint or restriction.

sections.heading: Establishment and membership of the Authority
sections.title: Establishment of Capital Markets Authority
sections.summary: This section establishes the Capital Markets Authority as a body corporate with perpetual succession, defining its powers to sue and be sued, acquire property, borrow money, enter contracts, and its composition. It specifies the appointment of a Chairperson and six members by the Cabinet Secretary, with requirements for expertise in legal, financial, banking, accounting, economics, or insurance matters.

requirements.text: There is hereby established an authority to be known as the Capital Markets Authority
The Authority shall be a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal
The Authority shall be capable in its corporate name of suing and being sued
The Authority shall be capable in its corporate name of taking, purchasing or otherwise acquiring, holding, charging and disposing of both movable and immovable property
The Authority shall be capable in its corporate name of borrowing and lending money
The Authority shall be capable in its corporate name of entering into contracts
The Authority shall consist of a Chairperson appointed by the President on the recommendation of the Cabinet Secretary
The Authority shall consist of six other members appointed by the Cabinet Secretary
The Authority shall consist of the Principal Secretary to the National Treasury or a person deputed by him or her in writing
The Authority shall consist of the Governor of the Central Bank of Kenya or a person deputed by him or her in writing
The Authority shall consist of the Governor of the Central Bank of Kenya or a person deputed by him or her in writing

sections.source_quote: 5. Establishment of Capital Markets Authority (1) There is hereby established an authority to be known as the Capital Markets Authority. (2) The Authority shall be a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal. (3) The Authority shall be capable in its corporate name of suing and being sued, taking, purchasing or otherwise acquiring, holding, charging and disposing of both movable and immovable property, borrowing and lending money, and entering into contracts. (4) The Authority shall consist of a Chairperson appointed by the President on the recommendation of the Cabinet Secretary, six other members appointed by the Cabinet Secretary, the Principal Secretary to the National Treasury or a person deputed by him or her in writing, and the Governor of the Central Bank of Kenya or a person deputed by him or her in writing.

sections.heading: Meetings and procedures of the Authority
Appointment of chief executive of the Authority
Objectives of the Authority
sections.title: Powers and functions of Capital Markets Authority
sections.summary: This section outlines the powers and functions of the Capital Markets Authority, including advising the Cabinet Secretary on capital markets, implementing government policies, employing officers, imposing sanctions, granting licenses, regulating collective investment schemes, and ensuring compliance with data protection principles under the Data Protection Act.

requirements.text: The Authority may advise the Cabinet Secretary on all aspects of the development and operation of capital markets
The Authority may implement policies and programmes of the Government with respect to the capital markets
The Authority may employ such officers and servants as may be necessary for the proper discharge of the functions of the Authority
The Authority may impose sanctions for breach of the provisions of this Act or the regulations made thereunder
The Authority may levy financial penalties proportional to the gravity or severity of the breach
The Authority may order a person to remedy or mitigate the effect of the breach, make restitution or pay compensation to any person aggrieved by the breach
The Authority may publish findings of malfeasance by any person
The Authority may suspend or cancel the listing of any securities or exchange-traded derivatives contracts for the protection of investors
The Authority may issue guidelines and notices on all matters within the jurisdiction of the Authority under this Act
The Authority may grant a licence to any person to operate as a stockbroker, derivatives broker, dealer or investment adviser
The Authority may grant approval to any person to operate as a securities exchange, commodity exchange, derivatives exchange
The Authority may regulate spot commodity markets
The Authority may recognize any person duly licensed by a prescribed foreign authority to carry on any licensed activity in Kenya which requires a license or an approval under this Act
The Authority may register, approve and regulate collective investment schemes
The Authority may license, approve and regulate private equity and venture capital companies that have access to public funds
The Authority may inquire into the affairs of any person which the Authority has approved or to which it has granted a licence
The Authority may give directions to any person which the Authority has approved or to which it has granted a licence
The Authority may conduct inspection of the activities, books and records of any persons approved or licensed by the Authority
The Authority may grant compensation to any investor who suffers pecuniary loss resulting from the failure of a licensed broker or dealer to meet his contractual obligations
The Authority may have recourse against any person whose act or omission has resulted in a payment from the Compensation Fund

The Authority may act as an appellate body in respect of appeals against any self regulatory organization securities or exchange-traded derivatives contracts exchange, derivatives exchange or central depository in actions by parties aggrieved thereby
The Authority may co-operate or enter into agreements for mutual co-operation with other regulatory authorities for the development and regulation of cross-border activities in capital markets
The Authority may regulate and oversee the issue and subsequent trading, both in primary and secondary markets, of capital market instruments
The Authority may regulate the use of electronic commerce for dealing in securities or offer services ordinarily carried out by a licensed person

The Authority may trace any assets, including bank accounts, of any person who, upon investigation by the Authority, is found to have engaged in any fraudulent dealings in an issuer and its securities or insider trading

The Authority may order caveats to be placed against the title to such assets or prohibit any such person from operating any such bank accounts as may be directed by the Authority, pending determination of any charges instituted against that person
The Authority may prescribe notices or guidelines on corporate governance of a company whose securities have been issued to the public or a section of the public
The Authority may ensure processing of personal data in the operations of capital markets is in accordance with principles set out under the Data Protection Act (Cap. 411C)

sections.source_quote: 11. Powers and functions of Capital Markets Authority (3) The Authority may, at any time revoke a delegation made under this section. (4) A delegation made under this section shall not prevent the Authority from performing the delegated function. (1) The Authority may delegate any of its functions under this Act to— (a) a committee of the Board; (b) a recognized self regulatory organization; or (c) an authorized person. (2) The Authority may, at any time revoke a delegation made under this section. (3) A delegation made under this section shall not prevent the Authority from performing the delegated function. (1) The Authority may delegate any of its functions under this Act to— (a) a committee of the Board; (b) a recognized self regulatory organization; or (c) an authorized person. (2) The Authority may, at any time revoke a delegation made under this section. (3) A delegation made under this section shall not prevent the Authority from performing the delegated function.

sections.heading: Financial year of Authority
sections.title: Authority's financial year
sections.summary: The Authority's financial year runs from July 1 to June 30 of each calendar year. This section defines the accounting period for the Authority's financial reporting.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines the financial year for the Authority, which may impact data retention and reporting timelines for financial data.

requirements.text: Authority's financial year commences on July 1

sections.source_quote: The financial year of the Authority shall be the period of twelve months commencing on the first day of July in each year.

sections.heading: Accounts
sections.title: Authority's accounts
sections.summary: The Authority must maintain proper books of accounts for its income, expenditures, assets, liabilities, and all transactions. This ensures transparency in financial operations.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed financial records, impacting data retention and reporting for financial data.

requirements.text: Authority shall keep proper books of accounts for income, expenditures, assets, liabilities, and transactions

sections.source_quote: The Authority shall cause proper books of accounts to be kept of its income and expenditures, assets and liabilities and all other transactions of the Authority.

The Compensation Fund shall consist of such sums of money as are paid under this Act as fines or penalties or under section 34 as ill-gotten gains where those harmed are not specifically identifiable
The Compensation Fund shall consist of such sums of money as accrue from interest and profits from investing Compensation Fund moneys

The Compensation Fund shall consist of such sums of money recovered by or on behalf of the Authority from entities whose failure to meet their obligations to investors result in payments from the Compensation Fund

sections.heading: Establishment of the Investor Compensation Fund (cont.)
Application for securities exchange, commodities exchange approval
sections.title: Changes in securities exchange rules
sections.summary: This section outlines the process for securities exchanges to amend their rules. Amendments require prior approval from the Authority, and the Authority must provide written notice within thirty days of receiving the amendments. Certain rule changes, such as fee adjustments or administrative matters, may take effect immediately upon filing with the Authority.
sections.dtia_summary: This section governs rule changes for securities exchanges, which may impact DTIAs through their influence on data handling practices and regulatory compliance.

requirements.text: The rules of an approved securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange, in so far as they have been approved by the Authority, shall not be amended, varied or rescinded without the prior approval of the Authority

Where the board of directors of an approved securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange wishes to amend its rules, it shall forward the amendments to the Authority for approval

The Authority shall, after hearing from the securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange, and within thirty days of receipt of a notice under subsection (2) give written notice to the securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange stating whether such amendments to the rules are allowed or disallowed and in the event of the rules being disallowed, the Authority shall give reasons for such disallowance

Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph (2), a proposed rule change may take effect upon filing with the Authority if designated by the exchanges as a stated policy, practice or interpretation with respect to the meaning, administration or enforcement of an existing rule

Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph (2), a proposed rule change may take effect upon filing with the Authority if designated by the exchanges as a proposal establishing or changing a fee or other charge

Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph (2), a proposed rule change may take effect upon filing with the Authority if designated by the exchanges as a proposal dealing solely with the administration of the exchange or other matters which the Authority may specify
In addition to the provisions of subsection (4), the Authority may add other items which it determines to be appropriate in fulfilling its objective under this Act
Subject to section 21B, the Authority may abrogate any rules made under section 21A if there exists risks that have not been adequately mitigated in the rules

sections.source_quote: 20. Changes in securities exchange, commodities exchange rules (1) The rules of an approved securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange, in so far as they have been approved by the Authority, shall not be amended, varied or rescinded without the prior approval of the Authority.

sections.heading: Changes in securities exchange, commodities exchange rules
Disciplinary action by securities exchange
sections.title: Disciplinary action by securities exchanges
sections.summary: Securities exchanges must notify the Authority within seven days of taking disciplinary action against trading participants or listed companies. The Authority may review such actions, affirm or set aside decisions after providing opportunities for hearing. The Authority can also take disciplinary action itself if an exchange fails to act, but must give prior notice.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires exchanges to report disciplinary actions to the regulator; regulator can override exchange decisions; regulator may directly discipline participants if exchanges fail to act.

requirements.text: Securities exchanges must notify Authority within seven days of disciplinary action against trading participants or listed companies
Authority may review disciplinary actions taken by exchanges after providing opportunity for hearing
Authority may take disciplinary action if exchange fails to act against participants

sections.source_quote: Where a securities exchange or a derivatives exchange reprimands, fines, suspends or expels, or otherwise takes disciplinary action against a trading participant or a listed company, it shall within seven days give notice to the Authority in writing, giving particulars including the name of the person, the reason for and nature of the action taken.

sections.heading: Licences required
sections.title: Licences required for financial activities
sections.summary: Individuals and entities must hold valid licenses from the Authority to operate as stockbrokers, dealers, fund managers, or other financial intermediaries. Exchanges and other entities must be approved by the Authority. License holders must comply with requirements and pay annual fees.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulator requires licenses for financial activities; license holders must comply with regulatory requirements and pay fees.

requirements.text: Individuals must hold valid licenses to operate as stockbrokers, dealers, fund managers, etc.
Exchanges and other entities must be approved by the Authority
License holders must comply with Authority requirements and pay annual fees

sections.source_quote: No person shall carry on business as a stockbroker, derivatives broker, REIT manager, trustee, dealer, investment adviser, fund manager, investment bank, central depository, authorised securities dealer, authorized depository, online forex broker, commodity dealer, commodity broker or hold himself out as carrying on such a business unless he or she holds a valid licence issued under this Act or under the authority of this Act.

sections.heading: Application for licence
sections.title: Application for licence procedures
sections.summary: Applications for licenses must be submitted in prescribed forms with fees; the Authority may require additional information. Licenses are granted only if applicants meet financial and other requirements. The Authority may impose conditions, vary conditions, or revoke licenses without prior hearing.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulator sets procedures for license applications; licenses require financial and operational compliance; licenses can be revoked without prior hearing.

requirements.text: Applications for licenses must be submitted in prescribed forms with fees
Authority may require additional information on applications
Licenses granted only if applicants meet financial and other requirements
Authority may impose conditions on licenses
Authority may vary conditions on licenses
Authority may revoke licenses without prior hearing

sections.source_quote: Applications for licenses must be submitted in prescribed forms with fees; the Authority may require additional information. Licenses are granted only if applicants meet financial and other requirements.

sections.heading: [Repealed by , s. 18.]
sections.title: Imposition of sanctions and penalties
sections.summary: The Authority may impose sanctions including public reprimands, suspensions, restrictions on licenses, recovery of benefits, financial penalties, and restitution orders for breaches of the Act. Penalties are recoverable summarily as civil debts.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulator can impose sanctions including suspensions, financial penalties, and restitution orders for breaches; penalties are recoverable as civil debts.

requirements.text: Authority may impose public reprimands for breaches
Authority may suspend trading for specified periods
Authority may restrict license use
Authority may recover benefits from breaches
Authority may impose financial penalties up to ten million shillings
Authority may order restitution for losses
Penalties are recoverable summarily as civil debts

sections.source_quote: The Authority may impose the following sanctions or levy financial penalties in accordance with this Act, for the breach of any provisions of this Act, the regulations, rules, guidelines, notices or directions made thereunder, or the rules of procedure of a securities, commodities or derivatives exchange, by a licensed or approved person, issuer, employee or a director of a licensed or approved person or employee director of a issuer as provided under section 11(3)(cc)

sections.heading: Suspension or revocation of a licence
sections.title: Conditions for suspending or revoking a license
sections.summary: The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who goes into liquidation, engages in unauthorized activities, has a receiver appointed, ceases business without approval, has directors not performing duties honestly, contravenes license conditions, fails to comply with directions, fails to provide information, provides false information, or is no longer fit to hold a license. Suspension cannot exceed three months and may be extended. The Authority must lift suspension after expiry or revoke the license. Failure to pay annual fees suspends the license, and non-payment within thirty days after suspension revokes it. The Authority must publish suspended or revoked licenses and provide an opportunity to be heard.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines conditions under which a license may be suspended or revoked, impacting data transfer operations by requiring licensees to maintain compliance with regulatory conditions before transferring data across borders.

requirements.text: The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if a licensed person goes into liquidation or an order is issued for winding up
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if a licensed person carries out activities outside the scope of licensed activities
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if a licensed person has a receiver or manager appointed on all or substantial part of company property
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if a licensed person ceases business for more than thirty days without Authority approval
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if any director or key employee has not performed duties honestly and fairly in the Authority's opinion
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if a licensed person contravenes or fails to comply with license conditions
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if a licensed person fails to comply with Authority directions
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if a licensed person fails to provide required information to the Authority
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if a licensed person provides false or misleading information
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if a licensed person is no longer fit and proper to hold a license
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license if a licensed person breaches any other provision under the Act
Suspension of a license may not exceed three months, but may be extended by the Authority for up to three months
The Authority must lift suspension or revoke the license at the end of the suspension period
Failure to pay the prescribed annual fee suspends the license
If a license is suspended and the licensee does not pay the fee within thirty days, the license is revoked
The Authority must publish suspended or revoked licenses in the Gazette
The Authority must provide an opportunity to be heard to persons affected by license suspension or revocation

sections.source_quote: 26. Suspension or revocation of a licence (1) The Authority may suspend or revoke a license, for such period or until the occurrence of such event as the Authority may specify, if a licensed person— (a) goes into liquidation or an order is issued for the winding up of the licensed person; (b) carries out any activity outside the scope of the licensed or approved activities; (c) has a receiver or a manager appointed on all or a substantial part of the property of the company; (d) ceases to carry on the licensed business for a period of more than thirty days unless it has obtained the approval of the Authority to do so; (e) any of its directors or key employees has not, in the opinion of the Authority, performed their duties honestly and fairly; (f) has contravened or failed to comply with any condition applicable in respect of the licence; (g) fails to comply with a direction of the Authority; (h) fails to provide the Authority with such information as it may require; (i) provides false or misleading information; (j) for any other reason, is no longer fit and proper person to hold a license; or (k) is in breach of any other provision under this Act.

sections.heading: Obligation to report changes
sections.title: Reporting changes to license registration
sections.summary: A licensed person must report to the Authority within fourteen days if they cease carrying on the business for which they were licensed or if there is a change in any particulars required to be entered in their license register. This ensures the Authority maintains up-to-date records of licensed entities and their activities.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires timely reporting of changes to license status, which impacts data transfer operations by ensuring licensees maintain compliance with regulatory requirements before transferring data across borders.

requirements.text: A licensed person must report to the Authority within fourteen days if they cease carrying on the business for which they were licensed
A licensed person must report to the Authority within fourteen days if there is a change in any particulars required to be entered in their license register

sections.source_quote: 28. Obligation to report changes Where— (a) the holder of a licence ceases to carry on the business to which the licence relates; or (b) a change occurs in any particulars which are required by section 27 to be entered in a register of licence holders with respect to the holder of a licence, the holder of the licence shall within fourteen days of the occurrence of the event concerned, give to the Authority, particulars of such event.

sections.heading: Licensing requirements
sections.title: Licensing requirements for financial entities
sections.summary: Before granting a license, the Authority must ensure the applicant is a legally constituted entity, has qualified directors, meets specific security requirements for stockbrokers, has administrative capacity, operates solely for clients (for stockbrokers), solely for their own benefit (for dealers), or both (for derivatives brokers), and complies with shareholding and director appointment limits to prevent undue control. The Authority also requires applicants to meet exchange admission criteria and pay admission fees.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes licensing requirements that impact data transfer operations by mandating specific governance and operational standards for entities handling financial data across borders.

requirements.text: The Authority must ensure the applicant is a legally constituted entity as prescribed in the Regulations or is duly constituted as a collective investment scheme
The Authority must ensure at least one director, chief executive officer, or person directing, conducting, managing, or supervising the business has satisfied minimum qualification requirements
In the case of a stockbroker, the applicant company must have lodged security in a prescribed sum with the securities exchange or Authority
The applicant company must have necessary administrative capacity to carry on business for which the license is required
In the case of a stockbroker's license, the applicant must carry on business solely on behalf of clients
In the case of a dealer's license, the applicant must carry on business solely on their own behalf
In the case of a derivatives broker license, the applicant may carry on business either on behalf of clients or on their own behalf, or both
A securities exchange or derivatives exchange must admit an applicant who fulfils all requirements imposed by the Authority and relevant exchange or self-regulatory organization
A securities broker, derivatives broker, or dealer whose license is revoked under section 26 must cease to be a trading participant of the securities exchange
An individual or corporate person shall not control or be beneficially entitled to more than thirty-three and a third percent of issued share capital or voting rights in a company
An individual or corporate person shall not appoint more than one-third of the members of the Board of directors
An individual or corporate person shall not receive more than thirty-three and a third percent of aggregate dividends and interest on shareholders loans in any given financial year
The provisions of subsection (4) shall not apply to a corporate entity licensed by a banking, insurance, pensions or securities regulator in Kenya

The provisions of subsection (4) shall not apply where the ownership structure of that corporate shareholder is diverse and no person holds or controls more than twenty-five percent of shares, votes, directorship appointments, dividends or interest on shareholder loans
A person who exercises control or is beneficially entitled to more than twenty-five percent of listed share capital or voting rights shall not be appointed as key personnel
A person who exercises control or is beneficially entitled to more than one quarter of the members of the Board of Directors shall not be appointed as key personnel
A person who exercises control or is beneficially entitled to more than twenty-five percent of aggregate dividends and interest on shareholders loans shall not be appointed as key personnel

sections.source_quote: 29. Licensing requirements (1) Before granting any licence or approval, the Authority in respect of a business that requires to be licensed or approved shall satisfy itself— (a) that the applicant is such legal entity as may be prescribed in the Regulations as the Authority may prescribe or is duly constituted as a collective investment scheme; (b) deleted by , s. 7; (c) that at least one the director, chief executive officer or such other person who directs, conducts, manages or supervises the business of the applicant has satisfied such minimum qualification requirements as may be prescribed; (d) in the case of a stockbroker, dealer or other person prescribed by the Authority that the applicant company has lodged security in such sum as may be determined by the Authority or an equivalent bank guarantee or bond with the securities exchange in which it is a trading participant or with the Authority or other person approved by the Authority as the case may be; (e) that the applicant company has the necessary administrative capacity to carry on business for which the licence is required; (f) in the case of an application for a stockbroker's licence, that the applicant shall carry on business solely on behalf of clients; (g) in the case of an application for a dealer's licence, that the applicant shall carry on business solely on their own behalf; (h) in the case of an application for a derivatives broker license, that the applicant may carry on business either on behalf of clients or on their own behalf, or both; (i) that the applicant is not a corporate entity licensed by a banking, insurance, pensions or securities regulator in Kenya; (j) that the ownership structure of that corporate shareholder is diverse and no person holds or controls more than twenty-five percent of shares, votes, directorship appointments, dividends or interest on shareholder loans.

sections.heading: Procedure for collective investment schemes
sections.title: Procedure for collective investment schemes
sections.summary: This section outlines the procedural requirements for collective investment schemes. It specifies the steps that must be followed when launching such schemes, including disclosure obligations and regulatory approvals. The process ensures transparency and compliance with capital markets regulations.

requirements.text: Collective investment schemes must follow specified procedures

sections.source_quote: 30. Procedure for collective investment schemes

sections.heading: Procedure for collective investment schemes (cont.)
sections.title: Procedures for listed company misconduct and corrective actions
sections.summary: This section outlines specific contraventions by listed companies that trigger corrective actions from the Authority. It covers failures to comply with the Act, providing false information, breaches of listing agreements, and contraventions of the Act itself. The Authority may issue cease and desist orders, specify actions to take or avoid, or address other matters it deems necessary. These provisions apply to listed companies and their obligations under the Capital Markets Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes conditions under which the Authority may intervene in listed companies' operations, relevant to DTIA for financial institutions involved in securities markets.

requirements.text: A listed company contravening, has contravened or is about to contravene any provision or requirement under this Act
A listed company has failed to comply with any provision of or requirement under this Act or rules of its listing exchange
A listed company has furnished the Authority with false, inaccurate or misleading information
There is a breach of the listing agreement in respect of any other securities
An issuer or other obligated party has furnished the Authority with false, inaccurate or misleading information
An issuer or offeror has contravened or is about to contravene a provision of this Act
The Authority may issue cease and desist orders to address breaches
The Authority may specify actions to take or avoid as part of corrective measures

sections.source_quote: Capital Markets Act (Cap. 485A) Kenya (c) a listed company is contravening, has contravened or is about to contravene any provision or requirement under this Act; (d) a listed company has failed to comply with any provision of or requirement under this Act or rules of its listing exchange; (e) a listed company has furnished the Authority with false, inaccurate or misleading information; (f) there is a breach of the listing agreement in respect of any other securities; (g) an issuer or other obligated party has furnished the Authority with false, inaccurate or misleading information; or (h) an issuer or offeror has contravened or is about to contravene a provision of this Act

sections.heading: Procedure for collective investment schemes (cont.)
sections.title: Obligations for restricted offers in securitisation transactions
sections.summary: This section mandates that false statements or omissions in prospectuses or applications for asset-backed securities must include all statements in prospectuses, offering memoranda, transaction documents, and credit rating agency communications. It also requires that continuing disclosure obligations apply to restricted offers. The provisions of sections 30D, 30E, 30F, 30JM, 30K, and 33O apply to restricted offers involving asset-backed securities. The Authority may exercise powers to make court applications for unfair prejudice or require records under this section.
sections.dtia_summary: Restricted offers involving asset-backed securities must comply with specific disclosure and continuing obligations, requiring thorough verification of statements and omissions to prevent misleading information across borders.

requirements.text: False statements or omissions in prospectuses or applications for asset-backed securities must include all statements in prospectuses, offering memoranda, transaction documents, and credit rating agency communications
False statements or omissions in prospectuses or applications for asset-backed securities must include continuing disclosure obligations under this Act
Sections 30D, 30E, 30F, 30JM, 30K, and 33O apply to applications for asset-backed securities in restricted offers
The Authority may make court applications for unfair prejudice under section 33C
The Authority may require production of records and documents under section 33D
The Authority may issue directions under section 30G

sections.source_quote: A reference in this Act to a false, untrue, misleading or deceptive statement in a prospectus or to the omission of information or a statement when making an application relating to or in connection with any securitisation transaction or any issue or offer of asset backed securities shall—

sections.heading: Register of interest in securities
sections.title: Register of interest in securities
sections.summary: This section requires the maintenance of a register of interests in securities. It mandates that entities track and disclose ownership interests to prevent insider trading and market manipulation. The register serves as a transparency mechanism for market participants.

requirements.text: Register of interest in securities must be maintained

sections.source_quote: 32. Register of interest in securities

sections.heading: Register of interest in securities (cont.)
sections.title: Fraudulent gain and insider trading penalties
sections.summary: This section establishes offences for obtaining financial or personal gain through fraud, deceit, or intentional concealment. It prohibits collusion with others to commit fraud and imposes penalties including fines and imprisonment for individuals, with companies facing higher penalties. The offences apply to any person who engages in fraudulent activities targeting the public, issuers, or regulated persons.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines specific fraudulent activities that could impact data transfers involving financial institutions, requiring robust safeguards to prevent misuse of sensitive financial information across borders.

requirements.text: Any person who on his own action or conspires with another by deceit, intentional concealment, omission or any fraudulent means to obtain financial or personal gain from the public, an issuer or a regulated person commits an offence.

sections.source_quote: 32KA. Obtaining gain by fraud Any person who on his own action or conspires with another by deceit, intentional concealment, omission or any fraudulent means to obtain financial or personal gain from the public, an issuer or a regulated person commits an offence.

sections.heading: Register of interest in securities (cont.)
sections.title: Statutory manager powers for financial institutions
sections.summary: This section grants the Capital Markets Authority the power to appoint a statutory manager to oversee a licensed person's affairs during crises, including financial instability or misconduct. The statutory manager must act diligently, preserve assets, recover debts, evaluate capital structure, and manage contracts. They also have authority to declare a moratorium on payments and report monthly to the Authority, with protections against liability for acts done in good faith.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates specific financial oversight mechanisms that could affect data transfers by requiring robust governance and accountability for financial institutions handling cross-border data.

requirements.text: The Authority may appoint a statutory manager to assume management, control and conduct of a licensed person's affairs to the exclusion of its board of directors.
The statutory manager must discharge duties with diligence and in accordance with sound investment and financial principles.
The statutory manager must trace and preserve all property and assets of the licensed person or its customers.
The statutory manager must recover all debts and other sums due to and owing to the licensed person.
The statutory manager must evaluate the capital structure and management of the licensed person and recommend restructuring.
The statutory manager may enter into contracts in the ordinary course of the business.
The statutory manager must obtain documents, records, accounts, statements or information relating to the licensed person's business.
The statutory manager may declare a moratorium on payment by the licensed person to all creditors equally.
The statutory manager may offset liabilities owed by the licensed person to any creditor against debts owed by that creditor.
The statutory manager may suspend the running of time for limitation of actions in respect of claims by creditors.
The statutory manager must furnish monthly reports to the Authority on activities.
The statutory manager may petition the High Court for winding up the licensed person if it is just and equitable.
The statutory manager must pay all costs and expenses out of the licensed person's assets in priority to other claims.

sections.source_quote: 33A. Powers of the Authority to intervene in management of a licence (1) This section shall apply and the powers conferred by subsection (2) may be exercised in the following circumstances—

sections.heading: Other offences
sections.title: Other offences under capital markets regulations
sections.summary: This section defines additional offences related to capital markets, including contravening provisions of the Act, publishing misleading information, or obstructing regulators. Offences are punishable by fines and imprisonment, with penalties increasing for subsequent offences. The section also covers compensation for losses caused by offences.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines offences that could impact data transfers by requiring accurate and transparent reporting of financial information.

requirements.text: Any person who contravenes provisions of this Act or rules, publishes misleading information, or obstructs regulators in their duties commits an offence.

sections.source_quote: 34. Other offences (1) Any person who— (a) contravenes any provision of this Act or any requirement imposed under the provision of this Act or any rule or regulation made thereunder; ...

sections.heading: Appeals from action by Authority
sections.title: Appeals from Authority decisions on securities licenses and compensation
sections.summary: Any person aggrieved by an Authority decision regarding license refusal, restrictions, suspension, revocation, public offer approval, securities listing, or compensation can appeal to the Capital Markets Tribunal within fifteen days. The Tribunal may require the Authority to show cause, affirm or set aside decisions after hearing, and award costs. The Tribunal has powers similar to the High Court for evidence and proceedings.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides a mechanism for challenging Authority decisions on securities licenses and compensation, relevant for DTIAs involving cross-border disputes over securities licenses or investor compensation.

requirements.text: Any person aggrieved by Authority decisions on license refusal, restrictions, suspension, revocation, public offer approval, securities listing, or compensation may appeal to the Capital Markets Tribunal within fifteen days.
The Tribunal may require the Authority to show cause for its action or decision.
The Tribunal may affirm or set aside an Authority decision after affording the Authority an opportunity to be heard.
The Tribunal shall award costs of proceedings before it and direct costs to be taxed according to a prescribed scale.

sections.source_quote: Any person aggrieved by any direction given by the Authority to such person or by a decision of the Authority or by the Investor Compensation Fund Board—refusing to grant a licence; imposing limitations or restrictions on a licence; suspending or revoking a licence; refusing to approve a public offer of securities; refusing to admit a security to the official list of a securities exchange; suspending trading of a security on a securities exchange; or requiring the removal of a security from the official list of a securities exchange—may appeal to the Capital Markets Tribunal against such directions, refusal, limitations or restrictions, cancellations, suspension or removal, as the case may be, within fifteen days from the date on which the decision was communicated to such person.

sections.heading: Directions and submission of reports
sections.title: Authority reporting obligations
sections.summary: The Cabinet Secretary may direct the Authority to provide returns, accounts, and information on its work. The Authority must submit annual reports with audited accounts within six months of the financial year-end, and the Cabinet Secretary must table these reports in Parliament within three months of submission.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires regular reporting from the Authority on its operations, relevant for DTIAs involving transparency and accountability in cross-border financial data handling.

requirements.text: The Cabinet Secretary may direct the Authority to furnish returns, accounts, and any other information with respect to the work of the Authority.
The Authority shall submit to the Cabinet Secretary a report of its operations and activities within six months after the close of each financial year, along with audited accounts.
The Cabinet Secretary shall table the report before Parliament within three months of its submission.

sections.source_quote: The Authority shall, within six months after the close of each financial year, submit to the Cabinet Secretary a report of its operations and activities throughout the year together with audited accounts in such form and detail as the Cabinet Secretary shall, from time to time, determine.
